lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I heard myself proclaim’d;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And by the happy hollow of a tree

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Escaped the hunt. No port is free; no place,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: That guard, and most unusual vigilance,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Does not attend my taking. Whiles I may ’scape,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: I will preserve myself: and am bethought

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: To take the basest and most poorest shape

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: That ever penury, in contempt of man,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Brought near to beast: my face I’ll grime with filth;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Blanket my loins: elf all my hair in knots;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And with presented nakedness out-face

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: The winds and persecutions of the sky.

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: The country gives me proof and precedent

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Of Bedlam beggars, who, with roaring voices,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Strike in their numb’d and mortified bare arms

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Pins, wooden pricks, nails, sprigs of rosemary;

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: And with this horrible object, from low farms,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Poor pelting villages, sheep-cotes, and mills,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Sometime with lunatic bans, sometime with prayers,

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: Enforce their charity. Poor Turlygod! poor Tom!

lines.speaker: EDGAR
lines.text: That’s something yet: Edgar I nothing am.

glosses.definition: lucky cavity in a tree — Edgar's hiding place
mad beggars discharged from Bethlem Hospital — the Elizabethan equivalent of discharged psychiatric patients roaming the countryside
a Bedlam beggar type — Edgar rehearses his Poor Tom persona
the archetypal Bedlam beggar name — Edgar's new identity
as Edgar I am nothing — the self-erasure
every harbor is watched — no exit from Britain
tangle into elf-locks — matted hair as if by fairies

glosses.phrase: elf all my hair in knots
glosses.definition: tangle hair into mats — a Bedlam-beggar trait

annotations.title: Edgar as Poor Tom — Bedlam beggars as historical type
annotations.body: Edgar's plan to disguise himself as a 'Bedlam beggar' draws on a real Elizabethan phenomenon. Bethlem Royal Hospital (Bedlam) in London housed the mentally ill; discharged patients — or people pretending to be discharged patients — were a recognized class of roadside beggar, sometimes called 'Abraham-men.' They used characteristic attire (pinned rosemary sprigs, mortified arms), characteristic speech (lunatic cursing, demon-naming), and sometimes exaggerated or faked their condition for alms. Edgar's 'Edgar I nothing am' adopts an extant social costume. Samuel Harsnett's 1603 Declaration of Egregious Popish Impostures — from which Shakespeare draws Poor Tom's demon names in 3.4 — was partly an exposure of faked demoniac possession by exorcists. Edgar's Poor Tom thus operates inside a real cultural argument Shakespeare was reading about.
sources: Samuel Harsnett, A Declaration of Egregious Popish Impostures (1603)

annotations.title: Edgar's self-erasure — 'Edgar I nothing am'
annotations.body: Edgar's twenty-one-line soliloquy is one of the play's quiet structural masterpieces. He disguises himself as 'Poor Turlygod, Poor Tom' and closes with 'Edgar I nothing am.' The phrase echoes the play's governing word ('nothing') and reassigns it to self-erasure rather than Cordelia's refusal or Lear's reduction. In 3.4 and 4.6 Edgar will do much of the play's work of moral witness while erased. Shakespeare's gamble is that the character who abolishes his identity is the one who can best observe — and who, at the end, may be the one to inherit the kingdom. Edgar's trajectory (disguise, witness, restorer) is the play's longest developmental arc, and it starts here.
sources: Jonathan Bate, Soul of the Age (2008)

trials.prompt: What historical social type is Edgar disguising himself as in 2.3?
options: A 'Bedlam beggar' — a discharged-or-pretending-to-be-discharged inmate of Bethlem Hospital (London), a recognized class of Elizabethan roadside beggar

trials.prompt: What is significant about Edgar's 'Edgar I nothing am'?
options: It echoes the play's governing word 'nothing' — now reassigned to self-erasure; Edgar becomes the most morally attentive witness precisely by abolishing his identity
